summary:
In cases like this one involving an acute myocardial infarct, the affected area would appear pale or white under TTC staining as it lacks viable cell structures necessary for uptake; conversely, healthy tissues turn red-brown from the reduction reaction triggered within living myocytes' intact mitochondria during normal metabolism. Agent1 concludes based on consistent scientific evidence regarding how TTC interacts differently between live vs non-viable cardiac muscle fibers post-infarction. ```plaintext
The damaged myocardium from a myocardial infarction appears pale or white when stained with TTC because those areas lack functional mitochondria required for the staining reaction. Healthy myocardium turns reddish brown due to successful reduction inside metabolically active cells’ mitochondria.
```
